title: Business financial data: March 2026 quarter | Stats NZ
content:
## Business financial data: March 2026 quarter
09 June 2026, 10:45am
Business financial data provides sales, purchases, salaries and wages, and operating profit estimates for most market industries in New Zealand, and information on stocks for selected industries. This collection uses a combination of survey, tax, and other administrative data.
- Business financial data: March 2026 quarter Excel spreadsheet, 68 KB

Excel spreadsheet, 68 KB

For all business financial data (BFD) industries, in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter:
- sales were $200 billion, up $10 billion (5.3 percent)
- purchases were $140 billion, up $6.4 billion (4.8 percent)
- salaries and wages were $32 billion, up $892 million (2.9 percent)
- operating profit was $29 billion, up $2.8 billion (11 percent).
When adjusting for seasonal effects, in the March 2026 quarter compared with the December 2025 quarter:
- sales rose in 11 of the 14 New Zealand Standard Industrial Output Classification (NZSIOC) level 1 industries
- the largest sales movements were in wholesale trade (up $1.5 billion); manufacturing (up $976 million); and retail trade and accommodation (up $683 million).
The business financial data release covers most market industries in the New Zealand economy, using survey and tax data.
New Zealand Standard Industrial Output Classification has more information on industry classifications.
The following table contains NZSIOC level 1 seasonally adjusted sales values, not adjusted for price changes.

Volumes are calculated by removing the effect of price changes from sales values.
In the March 2026 quarter, when adjusted for seasonal effects against the previous quarter:
- the volume of total manufacturing sales rose 3.6 percent, following a 0.4 percent fall in the December 2025 quarter
- the value of total manufacturing sales rose $976 million (2.8 percent), following a $286 million (0.8 percent) rise in the December 2025 quarter.
Changes in seasonally adjusted manufacturing sales ($), values and volumes, December 2025–March 2026 quarters
Meat and dairy products
Transport equipment, machinery, and equipment
Furniture and other manufacturing
Beverage and tobacco products
Fruit, oil, cereal, and other foods
Wood and paper products
Textile, leather, clothing, and footwear
Non-metallic mineral products
Petroleum and coal products
Chemical, polymer, and rubber products
In actual terms, the volume of finished goods stocks for total manufacturing rose 3.0 percent ($325 million) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.
In actual terms, total manufacturing purchases rose $1.1 billion (4.1 percent), and salaries and wages rose $103 million (2.1 percent) compared with the March 2025 quarter.
More seasonally adjusted manufacturing data can be found under Download data , or on Infoshare (under Industry sectors, Manufacturing Survey (ANZSIC06) – MFG).
When adjusted for seasonal effects, the total value of wholesale trade sales rose 3.8 percent ($1.5 billion) in the March 2026 quarter, following a 1.4 percent ($564 million) rise in the December 2025 quarter.
Changes in seasonally adjusted wholesale trade sales ($), by industry, December 2025–March 2026 quarters
Grocery, liquor, and tobacco products
Machinery and equipment
Motor vehicle and motor vehicle parts
The actual total value of wholesale trade stocks held at 31 March 2026 was $19 billion, up $497 million (2.8 percent) from 31 March 2025.
In actual terms, total wholesale trade purchases rose $2.8 billion (8.8 percent), and salaries and wages rose $94 million (3.8 percent) compared with the March 2025 quarter.
More seasonally adjusted wholesale trade data can be found under Download data , or on Infoshare (under Industry sectors, Wholesale Trade Survey – WTS).

## Further industry results
For the industry breakdowns below, only the sales values are seasonally adjusted. The purchases and salaries and wages values are actual data, so year-on-year movements are used for these measures.

### Forestry, fishing, and agricultural services
Total sales in this category were $3.7 billion, down $110 million (2.9 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $61 million (2.4 percent), and salaries and wages rose $17 million (2.9 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.
By industry the sales movements were:
- forestry and logging – down $57 million (4.1 percent)
- fishing, aquaculture, and support services for agriculture, forestry, and fishing – down $53 million (2.1 percent).
Total sales in this category were $1.6 billion, down $3.3 million (0.2 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases fell $9.9 million (1.3 percent), and salaries and wages rose $17 million (8.1 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Electricity, gas, water, and waste services
Total sales in this category were $6.2 billion, up $110 million (1.8 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases fell $1.2 billion (24 percent), and salaries and wages rose $28 million (4.4 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.
Total sales in this category were $24 billion, up $625 million (2.7 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $870 million (5.8 percent), and salaries and wages fell $15 million (0.4 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Retail trade and accommodation
Total sales in this category were $32 billion, up $683 million (2.2 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $1.3 billion (5.2 percent), and salaries and wages rose $136 million (3.1 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.
By industry the sales movements were:
- retail trade – up $483 million (1.9 percent)
- accommodation and food services – up $155 million (2.7 percent).
Retail trade survey: March 2026 quarter has more information on these industries.

### Transport, postal, and warehousing
Total sales in this category were $11 billion, up $65 million (0.6 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $392 million (6.3 percent), and salaries and wages rose $95 million (4.4 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Information media and telecommunications
Total sales in this category were $4.8 billion, down $490 million (9.2 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $48 million (1.9 percent), and salaries and wages fell $23 million (3.1 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Rental, hiring, and real estate services
Total sales in this category were $10 billion, up $239 million (2.4 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $389 million (8.3 percent), and salaries and wages rose $37 million (5.9 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Professional, scientific, technical, administrative, and support services
Total sales in this category were $22 billion, up $104 million (0.5 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $357 million (3.9 percent), and salaries and wages rose $102 million (1.7 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.
By industry the sales movements were:
- professional, scientific, and technical services – up $298 million (1.9 percent)
- administrative and support services – down $194 million (3.3 percent).

### Education and training
Total sales in this category were $1.1 billion, up $14 million (1.2 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $45 million (13 percent), and salaries and wages rose $41 million (9.1 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Health care and social assistance
Total sales in this category were $6.7 billion, up $115 million (1.7 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $173 million (6.9 percent), and salaries and wages rose $184 million (8.4 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

### Arts, recreation, and other services
Total sales in this category were $5.4 billion, up $93 million (1.8 percent) from the December 2025 quarter. Actual purchases rose $171 million (5.8 percent), and salaries and wages rose $77 million (6.3 percent) in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.
By industry the sales movements were:
- arts and recreation services – down $1.3 million (0.1 percent)
- other services – up $94 million (2.7 percent).
We received updated information or made improvements to our methodology for previous quarters, affecting results for the 12 quarters from March 2023 to December 2025. We have revised the data in the published series, and the affected series are marked with a revision flag in the published tables, CSV files, and relevant Infoshare series.
Infoshare changes by date has more information on the data updates and the series affected.
Use Infoshare to access time-series data for this release.
All series are found under the Subject category: Industry sectors.
Business financial data: Group: Business Data Collection – BDC
Manufacturing data: Group: Manufacturing Survey (ANZSIC06) – MFG
Wholesale trade data: Group: Wholesale Trade Survey – WTS
Selected services data: Group: Selected Services Survey (ANZSIC06) – SSS
